skill.name: lark-apps
skill.description: Deploy local HTML files or directories to Feishu Miaoda and get a public shareable URL. Manages app creation, updates, and access scope configuration.
skill.summary: Deploy local HTML files or directories to Feishu Miaoda and get a public shareable URL. Create apps, publish static websites, and configure access permissions.
skill.author: larksuite

security_audit.summary: Documentation-only skill for the lark-cli tool that deploys HTML to Feishu Miaoda. The 534 static findings are false positives: 'backtick execution' detections are markdown code blocks, 'credential file' references document the tool's built-in Validate feature that blocks credential publishing, and 'path traversal' findings are example paths in docs. No executable code exists in this skill.

low_findings.title: Extensive CLI command examples in documentation
low_findings.description: The skill documents multiple lark-cli commands (apps +create, +html-publish, +update, +access-scope-set, +access-scope-get) that interact with the Feishu platform API. All examples are within markdown code blocks and require the user's authenticated lark-cli binary. The skill includes safety guardrails: it instructs agents to read reference files before executing, and the tool has a Validate phase that blocks credential files.

low_findings.title: Documentation references credential file patterns as blocking targets
low_findings.description: The skill documents that the lark-cli's Validate phase scans for and blocks credential files (.env, .aws/credentials, .netrc, .git-credentials, .docker/config.json, .kube/config, .npmrc). This is a security feature preventing accidental credential publication, not a vulnerability. An --allow-sensitive flag exists for explicit educational/demos use cases.

content.value_statement: Easily publish local HTML files as shareable web apps on Feishu Miaoda. This skill handles app creation, content deployment, and access configuration in one workflow.
actual_capabilities: Create new Miaoda apps with a name and HTML type
Publish local HTML files or directories as accessible web apps
Update existing app names and descriptions
Configure app access scope (public, tenant, or specific users)
Retrieve current access scope configuration for an app
List and inspect existing Miaoda apps in your workspace
limitations: Only supports HTML app type currently (no SPA or native app support)
Requires lark-cli binary to be installed and authenticated
Cannot publish directories containing credential files without --allow-sensitive flag
Does not support searching or enumerating existing apps

use_cases.title: Quick static site deployment
use_cases.description: Deploy a built static site or HTML demo to a shareable URL in minutes. Ideal for sharing prototypes, landing pages, or documentation sites with team members or external stakeholders.

use_cases.title: Internal team tools
use_cases.description: Publish HTML-based forms, dashboards, or utilities for your organization. Configure tenant-wide access so all employees can use the tool without authentication friction.
use_cases.target_user: Operations teams and internal tool builders

use_cases.title: Educational content sharing
use_cases.description: Create and share HTML tutorials, interactive examples, or course materials as accessible web apps. Use specific access scope to share with particular students or groups.

prompt_templates.title: Deploy a local HTML file
prompt_templates.prompt: Deploy the file ./index.html to Feishu Miaoda as a new app called 'My Demo'

prompt_templates.title: Publish a static site directory
prompt_templates.prompt: Publish the ./dist directory to my existing Miaoda app with ID app_4k5jepcbjmv6m

prompt_templates.title: Create a public-facing app
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new HTML app called 'Survey Form' and make it publicly accessible to anyone on the internet
prompt_templates.scenario: Public app creation with access scope

prompt_templates.title: Restrict access to specific users
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an app called 'Team Dashboard' and configure it so only users with ou_id 'ou_abc123' and chat_id 'oc_xyz789' can access it
prompt_templates.scenario: Granular access control with specific targets

output: Application 'My HTML App' has been published.
To make this accessible to others, use apps +access-scope-set to configure sharing.

output_examples.input: Create a new app and set it to tenant-wide access
output: Created app 'Internal Tool' with ID app_4k5jepcbjmv6m.
Access scope set to tenant (all organization members).

best_practices: Always read the SKILL.md and relevant reference files before executing any lark-cli command
Run lark-cli auth login --domain apps once before your first operation to avoid repeated auth prompts
Use --dry-run flag to preview file manifests and verify content before publishing
Keep publish directories clean (exclude .git, node_modules) to avoid bundling unnecessary files
anti_patterns: Do not search for or enumerate existing apps - always create a new one unless the user provides an app_id
Do not pass absolute paths or paths with ../ to --path - use relative paths within the current working directory
Do not use --allow-sensitive unless the user explicitly intends to publish credential example files

faq.question: What is the difference between lark-apps and lark-drive?
faq.answer: lark-apps deploys HTML files as public web applications. lark-drive uploads files to Feishu cloud storage. Use lark-apps for shareable web content, lark-drive for file storage.

faq.question: Do I need to authenticate before using this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, run 'lark-cli auth login --domain apps' once before your first command. This grants the necessary scopes for all subsequent operations.

faq.question: Can I deploy a SPA or React app?
faq.answer: Currently only the HTML app type is supported. You can deploy pre-built static SPAs as HTML apps, but native SPA support may be added in the future.

faq.question: What happens if my directory contains .env files?
faq.answer: The lark-cli Validate phase will block the deployment and exit with an error. Either remove sensitive files from the directory or use --allow-sensitive if you intentionally want to publish credential examples.

faq.question: How do I find an existing app_id?
faq.answer: Ask the user to provide either the Miaoda app URL (like https://miaoda.feishu.cn/app/app_xxx) or the app_id string directly. The app_id starts with 'app_'.

faq.question: What is the access scope and when should I use each option?
faq.answer: Access scope controls who can view your app: 'public' for anyone on the internet, 'tenant' for all organization members, or 'specific' for selected users/groups only.
